calendar.matters:
Petitioner Caron Coates Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-26-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:James O'Neil for and with Caron Coates. Brian Coates in Propria Persona. Shari Jackquette in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that Mr. O'Neil has had a chance to review the tenative ruling and questioned if Mr. O'Neil would like to respond. Mr. O'Neil stated there there has been no opposition filed, that in the declerations filed by Shari she brings up the issue of the Child Protective Service claim and the issue of the ring and that there is no mention of the real estate claim. Furthermore Mr. O'Neil reported that the ring provided was the incorrect ring, that his client is asking to get the $5,000.00 back in exchange for the wrong ring and that and in regards to the real property there was an intent to harm the settlement agreement. Caron presented her oral argument in support of her position that she recived the wrong ring and the issues to the sale of the real property. Shari provided her rebuttal argument regarding her father's wedding ring. Brian provided case background regarding the sale of the real property.

Having heard oral argument and reviewing the court filed documents, the Court rules the tenative ruling as follows: The motion to enforce the settlement is denied. There is no showing of a breach via sale of the San Rafael property since the net proceeds of the sale were not in excess of $765,0000. Further, there is no showing that the wrong wedding ring was delivered, and the CPS report, if in fact instigated by Respondents, was not a part of or related to the settlement agreement. Reasonable attorneys fees, not in excess of $900.00 will be awarded to Respondent Coates along with $428.24 in costs, should he personally appear at the hearing. The Court to prepare a form of order.

Judge John K. Stewart, Clerk:N. Wong, Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. Not Reported.

Petition For Removal Of Shari Coates Jacquett As Successor Trustee; For Appointtment Of Caron Coates As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-25-2016 Off Calendar

Petition For Removal Of Shari Coates Jacquett As Successor Trustee; For Appointtment Of Caron Coates As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Aug-16-2016 Continued To Oct-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Rueppel Fror and with petitioner Caron Coates; Eddie Broitman and Amy Shelf for Shari Coates.

The Court noted his concern regarding family conflict as demonstrated in the submissions to the Court, and inquired regarding the possibility of mediation. Mr. Broitman agreed that mediation would be helpful. Mr. Rueppel confirmed that his client was also willing to give mediation a try. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than October 14, 2016. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by October 19, 2016. The matter was continued to and the Parties are to return on October 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)
